recommendations.name: AWS Batch
recommendations.description: Fully managed batch processing at any scale
recommendations.considerations: For your ETL use cases, you might consider using AWS Glue. For other batch-oriented use cases, including some ETL use cases, AWS Batch might be a better fit

recommendations.name: Amazon EC2
recommendations.description: Secure and resizable compute capacity in the cloud. Launch applications when needed without upfront commitments

recommendations.name: Amazon ElastiCache
recommendations.description: Fully managed in-memory data store, compatible with Redis or Memcached. Power real-time applications with sub-millisecond latency.

recommendations.name: Amazon Elastic Container Service (ECS)
recommendations.description: Highly secure, reliable, and scalable way to run containers
recommendations.considerations: The applicability of this recommendation will depend on your organization's container strategy, and which target is recommended as part of this strategy: Virtual machines, Individual containers, Kubernetes clusters, etc. Your container strategy will also inform how processes and people impact the technology choices, if for example a DevOps strategy or a Microservices strategy exists.

recommendations.name: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS)
recommendations.description: Managed service to start, run, and scale Kubernetes
recommendations.considerations: The applicability of this recommendation will depend on your organization's container strategy, and which target is recommended as part of this strategy: Virtual machines, Individual containers, Kubernetes clusters, etc. Your container strategy will also inform how processes and people impact the technology choices, if for example a DevOps strategy or a Microservices strategy exists.

recommendations.name: Amazon RDS for Oracle
recommendations.description: Set up, operate, and scale an Oracle database in the cloud with just a few clicks

recommendations.name: Amazon S3
recommendations.description: Object storage built to store and retrieve any amount of data from anywhere

recommendations.name: Amazon Simple Queue Service
recommendations.description: Fully managed message queues for microservices, distributed systems, and serverless applications

recommendations.name: Red Hat OpenShift Service on AWS
recommendations.description: Managed OpenShift integration in the cloud. Red Hat OpenShift Service on AWS (ROSA) provides an integrated experience with OpenShift. You can use the wide range of AWS compute, database, analytics, machine learning (ML), networking, mobile, and other services to build secure and scalable applications faster.

recommendations.name: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)

recommendations.name: Azure Red Hat OpenShift
recommendations.description: Build, deploy, and scale apps on OpenShift with confidence. Azure Red Hat OpenShift provides highly available, fully managed OpenShift clusters on demand, monitored and operated jointly by Microsoft and Red Hat. Kubernetes is at the core of Red Hat OpenShift. OpenShift brings added-value features to complement Kubernetes, making it a turnkey container platform as a service (PaaS) with a significantly improved developer and operator experience.

recommendations.name: Oracle Base Database Service
recommendations.description: Oracle Base Database Service enables you to maintain absolute control over your data while leveraging the combined capabilities of Oracle Database and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI). OCI offers single-node DB systems and multi-node RAC DB systems on virtual machines
